description: You are a dialogue editor for a documentary film. The interview recording at /home/ga/Audio/interview_raw/ was recorded with two microphones (Boom and Lavalier). However, due to a miswired cable, the Lavalier mic was recorded 180 degrees out of phase, causing severe phase cancellation when played together with the Boom mic. Additionally, the Boom mic picked up unwanted background hum between speech phrases. Repair the audio by importing 'boom_raw.wav' and 'lav_raw.wav' into tracks named 'Boom Mic' and 'Lavalier Mic' respectively. Fix the phase cancellation on the Lavalier Mic track and apply a noise gate or expander to the Boom Mic track to remove the background hum. Export the repaired stereo mix to /home/ga/Audio/interview_fixed/dialogue_mix.wav.
